body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 39/2026
Made under: Workers Compensation Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 19, 2026

B.C. Reg. 39/2026, deposited March 19, 2026, under theWORKERS COMPENSATION ACT[sections 110 and 117]. Resolution of the Workers’ Compensation Board, dated March 17, 2026. The Workers’ Compensation Board orders that, effective October 1, 2026, the Occupational Health and Safety Regulation, B.C. Reg. 296/97, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix. — W. L. CRONK,Chair, Board of Directors. 1	Section 31.1 of the Occupational Health and Safety Regulation, B.C. Reg. 296/97, is amended (a)	by renumbering the section as section 31.1 (1), (b)	in subsection (1) by repealing paragraph (b) of the definition of"fire department"and substituting the following: (b)	a board, society or commission on behalf of a body referred to in paragraph (a), or, (c)	in subsection (1) by repealing the definition of"firefighter", (d)	in subsection (1) by adding the following definition: "fire personnel"means workers, other than firefighters, who are employed, with or without remuneration, in firefighting, fire inspection, fire investigation, the maintenance of firefighting equipment, the training for and direction of those activities, or other similar duties;, and (e)	by adding the following subsection: (2)	In this Part, a reference to a firefighter is to be read as including fire personnel. 2	Section 31.2 is repealed and the following substituted: 31.2This Part does not apply to forest fire fighting. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC expands workplace safety rules to cover fire personnel beyond firefighters
minutes_briefs.summary: The Workers' Compensation Board has amended the Occupational Health and Safety Regulation to broaden the definition of who is covered under fire safety rules. Starting October 1, 2026, the term 'firefighter' will also include 'fire personnel' — workers who do firefighting, fire inspection, fire investigation, equipment maintenance, training, or similar duties, whether paid or unpaid. This change means more workers will have the same workplace safety protections as firefighters.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your workplace safety policies to ensure all fire personnel are included under the same protections as firefighters by October 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC fire personnel safety regulation takes effect

minutes_briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Natural Resources
